_comment: ADR-0008 point 5 risk-classification mapping: verdict-gate.yml classifies
a PR's changed paths against these ordered rules (internal/gate.ClassifyChangedPaths,
internal/gate.PathRule) to decide codex-only (non-critical) vs codex+independent-Claude
N-of-2 (critical). Rules are evaluated in array order with last-match-wins
(same semantics as .github/CODEOWNERS resolution) — a later, narrower rule
overrides an earlier, broader one for a specific path.
This file is enforcement-integrity-protected (ADR-0011 point 11, point 4(b)):
it is loop-authored-PR-changeable content that defines the *sole* automated
merge gate's scrutiny level, so it must not be movable/editable outside the
same CODEOWNERS + internal/enforcement.sacredRequiredPaths protection this
PR wires up for it — see .github/CODEOWNERS and
internal/enforcement/codeowners.go.
ADR-0008 point 5: 'risk:critical 분류는 ... enforcement 계층의 경로 기반
매핑이 기계적으로 계산하며, 매핑에 없는 경로의 기본값은 critical'. The
leading '**': critical' rule below encodes that default explicitly — every
other rule either narrows it (non-critical carve-outs) or re-asserts it for
a subtree nested under a carve-out (e.g. docs/adr/** staying critical even
though docs/** is non-critical).
Refining this mapping (adding narrower carve-outs, moving a package from
one class to the other) is expected as the codebase grows — it does not
require an ADR amend, only the same CODEOWNERS-protected PR review every
change to this file already gets.
